full_text:
Hey everyone! If you’re into AI and automation, you know that speed and efficiency are everything. Enter programmatic tool calling! . This feature lets Claude execute code directly with external tools, slashing latency like a hot knife through butter. . Imagine running a multi-tool workflow without the annoying round trips to the model. .

So, how does this magic happen? Well, programmatic tool calling minimizes token consumption by filtering data before it even hits the model’s context window. . Think about it: less data means faster processing! . But hold on, to make this work, you need to enable the code execution tool. .

Let’s bring this to life with a real-world example. Imagine querying sales data across multiple regions. With programmatic tool calling, Claude can aggregate the results in one go! . Here’s how it works: you would use a cURL command to make the API call specifying headers and data structure. . This example uses the model 'claude-opus-4-6' with a token limit of 4096. .

Now, let’s dive deeper into how the API works. The tools array in your API request specifies the type and name of the tool for code execution. . This allows for a seamless integration with various external tools. . Keep in mind, though, that the data retention policy is standard and not covered by Zero Data Retention (ZDR). .

So, why should you care about programmatic tool calling? Because it’s a game changer for any developer or business that needs to optimize workflows. . The reduction in latency and token usage means you can get more done in less time. . So, get out there, enable that code execution tool, and start harnessing the power of programmatic tool calling today! .
